authoritative_sources.title: Reviewing changes in pull requests
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub official documentation defines PR review comments, approvals, requested changes, dependencies, and feedback incorporation in a common software delivery record.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: review intake, finding status, requested changes, approval boundary, resubmission evidence, and closure dependencies
practical_use_in_pack: scenario design for structured review and hold cases
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes pull request review, required review, or approval behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: About code owners
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub official documentation defines CODEOWNERS behavior used for ownership routing, required review expectations, and review authority boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ownership lookup, reviewer routing, code-owner approval dependency, and refusal to replace authorized reviewers
used_for: approval records and systems of record
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes CODEOWNERS or required review behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Actions Workflows
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub official documentation defines workflow, job, trigger, and repository evidence behavior for a common CI quality-gate system.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: CI evidence retrieval, quality gate interpretation, workflow run references, and pipeline handoff
practical_use_in_pack: CI run and workflow evidence expectations
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes workflow syntax, runner behavior, artifacts, or security recommendations.

authoritative_sources.title: Configuring retention for GitHub Actions artifacts and logs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub official documentation defines retention options for workflow artifacts and logs, which are common review and quality evidence workpapers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence retention, audit-trail packet design, client delivery, and retention-conflict escalation
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes artifact and log retention behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: git-diff
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Git official documentation defines diff output, options, and changed-content representation used as the primary review artifact.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: diff evidence interpretation, changed-file scope, patch context, and hunk-level finding traceability
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or when Git changes diff format, options, or documentation materially.

authoritative_sources.title: Google Engineering Practices Documentation - The Standard of Code Review
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google publishes a recognized public engineering-practices guide for scalable code review, reviewer judgment, and maintainability tradeoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: review standard, maintainability expectations, reviewer decision framing, and defect-versus-preference separation
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or when Google updates public engineering practices guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 25010:2023 Systems and software quality models
authoritative_sources.publisher: International Organization for Standardization / International Electrotechnical Commission
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO/IEC 25010 is an international quality model reference for software product and system quality characteristics used to structure maintainability and reliability criteria.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality attribute selection, maintainability risk classification, acceptance criteria review, and control objective framing
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on ISO/IEC revision, amendment, or replacement publication.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-218 Secure Software Development Framework Version 1.1
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SSDF is official U.S. federal secure software development guidance covering verification, peer review, vulnerability response, evidence discipline, and secure release practices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: security-aware review, verification evidence review, defect remediation, and vulnerability handoff
practical_use_in_pack: boundary between code review support and security risk acceptance
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or when NIST updates SSDF, federal secure software guidance, or related SP 800 series guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Code Review Guide
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP publishes a recognized public guide for structured security-focused code review, vulnerability-oriented findings, and secure review discipline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: security-relevant review prompts, input validation, output encoding, authorization, logging, and escalation to security specialists
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when OWASP publishes a major guide update.

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Application Security Verification Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP ASVS is a recognized public verification standard for application security controls that reviewers can use without becoming security risk approvers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: security-adjacent acceptance criteria, negative cases, verification control references, and security-specialist escalation triggers
practical_use_in_pack: authz, validation, logging, and data protection review prompts
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when OWASP ASVS publishes a new release or material verification-level change.

authoritative_sources.title: Common Weakness Enumeration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: MITRE maintains CWE as a common classification for software weakness types used to normalize security and quality defect language.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: defect taxonomy, weakness classification, repeat-defect pattern naming, and handoff clarity for security-adjacent findings
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when MITRE publishes material CWE taxonomy updates.

authoritative_sources.title: OpenAPI Specification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The OpenAPI Initiative publishes the authoritative public specification for HTTP API contract documents, operations, schemas, security schemes, and reusable components.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API contract artifact review, compatibility assessment, schema-driven negative cases, and evidence requirements
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when the OpenAPI Initiative publishes a new minor or major version.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 9110 defines normative HTTP method, status, representation, and semantic behavior for API correctness assertions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: HTTP API behavior assertions, protocol edge cases, status-code checks, idempotency, and retry expectations
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval, IETF errata, or successor RFC publication.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 2119: Key words for use in RFCs to Indicate Requirement Levels
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: RFC 2119 standardizes requirement language used to interpret MUST, SHOULD, MAY, and prohibition terms in acceptance criteria and contracts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: acceptance criteria interpretation, review gate wording, and ambiguity escalation
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval, IETF errata, or replacement by updated BCP guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: PEP 8 - Style Guide for Python Code
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Python Software Foundation publishes PEP 8 as official Python style guidance that affects readability and maintainability review when tenant code is Python.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: stack-specific readability, naming, layout, and maintainability expectations when tenant projects use Python
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Python style guidance materially changes.

authoritative_sources.title: Node.js Test Runner
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Node.js publishes official runtime test runner documentation for JavaScript projects using built-in test execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: stack-specific test execution evidence and runtime-specific test behavior when tenant projects use Node.js
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when Node.js updates active LTS test runner behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACM is a major professional computing society and its ethics code covers quality, harm avoidance, privacy, confidentiality, competence, and duty boundaries for computing professionals.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: confidentiality, professional judgment, client-delivery evidence handling, harm escalation, and refusal to exceed authority
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on ACM code revision.

workflow_stages.name: Review intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm request intent, software artifact, delivery context, tenant facts, authority sources, confidentiality terms, approval matrix, retention rules, and output shape before deciding whether the request is in scope, blocked, refused, or escalated.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Quality-review or confidentiality handling references

workflow_stages.name: Authoritative evidence retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Retrieve and reconcile diffs, changed files, requirements, acceptance criteria, design notes, API contracts, schemas, test inventories, CI runs, logs, artifacts, coverage, defects, incidents, review notes, code-owner rules, client commitments, and approval records before issuing a review status.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation
Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation

workflow_stages.name: Control objective and inspection application
workflow_stages.description: Apply documented review standards, maintainability criteria, software quality attributes, acceptance criteria, API/protocol expectations, security-adjacent verification controls, and tenant merge gates to available evidence.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
Public security and software quality guidance

workflow_stages.name: Finding classification and remediation planning
workflow_stages.description: Classify findings by correctness, maintainability, testability, regression, performance, security-adjacent, compatibility, documentation, or evidence risk; assign severity, confidence, owner, remediation, and retest expectation.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Quality-review or confidentiality handling references
Public security and software quality guidance

workflow_stages.name: Defect, regression, and corrective-action handling
workflow_stages.description: Classify repeated defects, regressions, incidents, reopened bugs, flaky tests, missing RCA, incomplete retest evidence, nonconformances, and corrective-action gaps while preserving incident command, security, legal, and release approval boundaries.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Quality-review or confidentiality handling references
Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation

workflow_stages.name: Disposition and hold or merge recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence-backed pass, fail, changes-requested, hold, merge-ready-recommended, investigation-needed, nonconforming, or escalation-required recommendations without claiming final signoff, merge, release, or bypass authority.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Quality-review or confidentiality handling references

workflow_stages.name: Client delivery, confidentiality, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package sanitized review evidence, record assumptions, handle retention and recipient authorization, and escalate when client rights, professional judgment, legal/privacy, security risk acceptance, contractual signoff, code-owner, or release authority determines the answer.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Quality-review or confidentiality handling references

artifacts: Pull request, merge request, patch set, diff, commit range, changed-file inventory, code area map, review comment, requested-changes record, reviewer approval, branch protection status, code owner rule, and resubmission evidence
Issue, user story, acceptance criteria, design document, ADR, API contract, schema, event contract, dependency map, migration note, and release note

Unit, integration, API, contract, regression, UI, property, performance, and security-adjacent tests; coverage reports; CI workflow runs; test reports; logs; traces; screenshots; benchmark results; and artifact links
Defect, incident, bug history, flaky-test register, RCA, corrective-action record, nonconformance, remediation owner, retest proof, and unresolved-risk register

Structured review note, severity matrix, confidence rating, finding list, refactor plan, prioritized action list, hold or merge-readiness recommendation, quality or safety review note, control or incident summary, and corrective-action escalation
Client engagement scope, confidentiality terms, approved recipient list, retention settings, approval matrix, client commitment, document repository reference, and delivery packet
systems_of_record: Source repository and pull request or merge request review system
Branch protection, code owner, review policy, and approval records
Requirements, backlog, design document, ADR, API contract, schema registry, and acceptance criteria systems
CI/CD workflow system, test report store, coverage dashboard, artifact store, benchmark store, and release checklist
Test management system, defect tracker, incident management system, RCA tracker, corrective-action tracker, quality risk register, and flaky-test register
Document management, engagement scope, client commitment, confidentiality, approval, retention, and policy records

allowed: Classify code review requests as in-scope, blocked, hold, merge-ready-recommended, changes-requested, investigation-needed, nonconforming, escalated, or refused.
Recommend review findings, severity, confidence, remediation order, refactor sequence, test additions, edge-case coverage, evidence requirements, and corrective actions from named records.

Prepare structured review notes, prioritized action lists, low-confidence escalations, out-of-scope refusals, quality or safety review notes, control or incident summaries, and corrective-action escalations.
State assumptions, uncertainty, missing evidence, source freshness, and approval dependencies.
Route work to adjacent specialists or human reviewers when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required facts, evidence, systems of record, authority sources, approval records, code-owner records, retention rules, or confidentiality terms are missing, stale, or contradictory.
Public source guidance and tenant policy conflict without documented precedence.
Client rights, professional judgment, legal/privacy interpretation, security risk acceptance, release approval, contractual signoff, code-owner approval, or regulated duty determines the answer.
A repeat defect, incident, nonconformance, regression, or corrective action lacks RCA, owner, mitigation, retest evidence, or closure support.
A client-delivery review evidence packet has unresolved recipient, retention, redaction, confidentiality, source disclosure, vulnerability disclosure, or approval conflict.
refuse: Bypass hold, release, safety, change, incident, review, branch-protection, code-owner, confidentiality, retention, approval, or quality-control boundaries without explicit authority.
Approve releases, approve merges, clear holds, close findings, sign contracts, publish client evidence, accept legal/privacy risk, accept security risk, or provide final attestation.

Own implementation code changes, cloud/network/identity/platform architecture, environment provisioning, incident command, platform operations, runner operations, direct capacity planning, procurement, product strategy, GTM strategy, pricing, or executive roadmap planning.
Perform full penetration testing, threat hunting, security policy enforcement, secrets governance, vulnerability management ownership, or security risk acceptance.
Disclose tenant, client, source, test, defect, vulnerability, incident, credential, production, contractual, or quality evidence records to unapproved recipients or channels.

domain_failure_modes: Generic code review advice that lacks traceable files, diff hunks, requirements, tests, CI, defects, ownership, or policy evidence.
Treating style preference as a blocking defect without tenant standard, maintainability risk, or evidence support.
Clearing a hold, merge gate, finding, nonconformance, or corrective action without sufficient workpaper support or authorized reviewer evidence.
Treating a review recommendation as final merge approval, release approval, code-owner approval, contractual signoff, or attestation.

Overreaching into implementation ownership, platform operations, incident response, CI/CD execution, product strategy, cloud architecture, security risk acceptance, privacy legal interpretation, penetration testing, or procurement.
Missing repeat-defect signals because incidents, reopened bugs, flaky tests, RCA, and retest proof are not reconciled.
Using stale or stack-inappropriate public guidance when tenant runtime, framework, CI, contract, repository convention, or policy context materially changes the answer.
Publishing client or tenant review evidence without approved recipient, retention, redaction, confidentiality, engagement-scope, and approval support.
Confidently synthesizing conflicting public source, tenant policy, client commitment, review rule, or approval records instead of escalating.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.
Client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness.
Escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action.

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline includes Python and Node.js examples, but tenant runtime and framework versions must be retrieved before stack-specific review guidance is final.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant policy precedence over public baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The spec requires escalation when public guidance and tenant policy conflict without documented precedence; no blocking ambiguity remains for the validated public spec.

notes: Research contract version 2026-04-08 is satisfied for the professional-and-information-services domain pack.
Required source classes are covered and each source records authority rationale, workflow usage, refresh interval, decay policy, and review date.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack use.
